reviews.review_text:
Overall was a great value option if you want to stay at a cabin. I saw some reviews which were raised a few concerns but none of those were issues I faced whilst staying there.

Everything was clean within the cabins and public bathrooms were mostly good, apart from some other members forgetting to flush toilets or keeping the sink running (male)

Kitchen worked fine. Don't expect a 5 star cooking experience but was able to cook relatively easily without issue.

Showering without hot water was fine, but remember to bring a dollar if you can't handle cold water.

Only minor inconviences were that the cabin gets hot throughout the day, easily fixed with the solid ceiling fan and the fact that you'll probably be in town during the day. Some bug windows requiring some repairs and limited outlets to charge stuff.

Other than that everything was great, beds were comfortable, great curtains, lots of amenities you can expect from an accommodation like this all for a reasonable price of $125pn for the cheapest cabin

I highly recommended accommodation as long as you're aware of the limitations of going for the value/shared option and slightly further away from the Jervis Bay area.

From the moment we arrived the hosts were warm, welcoming, and incredibly helpful, making sure we had everything we needed. You can tell they genuinely care about creating a memorable experience for their guests.||The cabin was clean, spacious and beautifully maintained, with an easy to use BBQ on the terrace. The whole site was incredibly peaceful and full of birds and wildlife. Its also perfectly located, just a short drive from the amazing beaches. ||If you’re looking for a relaxing nature getaway with privacy, beauty, and convenience, Hidden Campsite is absolutely perfect!

Loved every moment at Hidden Creek, nothing but good vibes surrounded by Mother Nature in a beautiful setting. Highly recommend, would love to come back, hopefully will. Here with my 13 year old daughter and friends in the cabins, made some great memories.

Location is convenient, so close to Huskisson. Check in smoothly in the office, Tracey was so nice to give all tourist information around the area. The cabin is cozy and clean. The campsite is very quiet during night time. Parking just next to my cabin.
A streetlight right above the tent pitch, extremely loud neighbours, lunatics starting a smoky fire at 7:00 AM. Over rated and quite expensive campsite. You have to pay for a hot shower.

The cabins are perfectly located in the woods, but very close to huskissons. Coming by car is recommended, because going to the shops and/or national parks is more easily. The service was very good, the owners are super nice and helpful! They gave us great advice about things to do in and around the area. The cabin was spacious and big, which was super convenient. The only thing is that there is not an oven, which I thought I read about in the descriptions, but I'm not entirely sure. We loved to stay here and would absolutely recommend this to anyone!
It was a great, cosy little cabin situation in a nice bush area.  Reasonable prices, friendly staff, lovely animals (peacocks and ducks).
The place is very peaceful, clean and perfect for a camping vibe.
The route is convenient, the staff is friendly and helpful, the room is very clean, the surrounding atmosphere is quiet, and it is close to the beautiful beach

Be prepared to be greeted by a beautiful peacock as soon as you enter the premises |A thousand trees with just as much native wildlife. Countless possums, kangaroos, parrots, a wombat and even a couple of gliders.|Our cabin accommodation was spotlessly clean and inviting, then proved to be just as comfortable.|There is a small bat ramp in the camping area which is ok for a small tinny to be launched |The staff are extremely friendly and simply can't do enough to make you feel welcome |We only stayed for a weekend but will now be returning for a longer holiday

What a relaxing few days after the hectic time of Christmas. Sitting by the campfire alongside the creek, listening to the sounds of the birds and trees. |Visited by wallabies and being with nature. Kayaking down the creek, so surreal. |This campsite is a must. Just one with nature. |A local band playing in Huskisson and enjoying the spectacular white Sandy beaches and swimming the warm ocean waters.

Another amazing weekend spent at Hidden Creek Campsite. The tranquility of the campsite is perfect after a day exploring the many white sandy beaches. Jervis Bay is a picturesque gem with turquoise waters and crisp white sand and definitely the place to escape the busy city life.

The best campsite we have stayed at on our travels from Cairns to Melbourne! So pretty and peaceful. |Very friendly staff who provided a list of places to stop on the rest of the way to Melbourne. |Price was also very good compared to some of the worse, touristy spots we have stayed at costing much nore. Will definitely be back :))

One of the attractions of coming here, is that it is one of the few places in the area that has an EV Charger. We have a BYD Atto 3, and it charges at @ 7kw/h. It may not be as fast as some others, but at least it's 24/7.

Other sites, I have stayed at turn off the EV charging at @6PM, which is not great if you turn up with a low battery, and need to head out again.

We stayed on an Ensuite site, which could benefit with some hanging hooks for towels. The site seems sheltered from wind, which is also great.

Staff are friendly.

We were Provided with a map, showing arrows in regards to vehicle flow. The Site should put signs on the road to reflect this so that all vehicles try to adhere to this, as well as any kids etc riding carts, scooters, bikes etc.

**********************************************************
A curfew should be put in place for kids riding around the park, etc
**********************************************************
Great park, magnificent facilities and the staff go above and beyond to ensure you have a great stay, we highly recommend this park.
Great park, magnificent facilities and the staff go above and beyond to ensure you have a great stay, we highly recommend this park.

I was very pleased with the service and overall thing at Tasman. The staff were friendly and helpful. The place is close to nature and the river flowing close to the place was even better. I loved the place. Overall the stay was cozy and pleasant. Loved it.

Only my suggestion that if the washroom could be close to the Bushman Cabin.

Was looking forward to the trip as really love Jervis Bay. Came here with 3 teen boys, my mum and a friend for 4 nights in the 2nd week of the April 2026 school holidays. We paid over $450 a night for a waterfront villa which we were happy to do as we love the area and really wanted a relaxing holiday. HOWEVER - please, please explain to me why the holiday park is configured in this way: you have a row of waterfront villas, all of which all the bedrooms look out to/face the very noisy, overflowing camping area of families and children. Not fun. And in no way conducive to sleep, peace & quiet or any relaxing to be done whatsoever. Great if you want to hear adults getting drunk way past midnight or the sounds of kids screaming on their scooters and dinkies doing laps of the campsite between 6am - 7am (true!). Perhaps put all the families and kids in the largely vacant camping area on the other side of the park instead of right underneath the sleeping quarters/bedrooms of the lakefront villas. Make that make sense. And yes, I understand kids make noise and I understand it's the school holidays - but other people who stay here have the right to a nice holiday and SLEEP. We paid a premium to stay in a holiday park. Note: apparently you are not supposed to make any 'undue noise' between 10pm - 6am. FYI - we also heard the same sentiment from other people who were also staying here. Not everyone complains - some people don't think they can say anything and others just don't come back.

This is a great place. Awesome location and an easy 1 hr 45 minute drive South of Sydney. The site is very clean & tidy and very well equipped e.g. pool, play area for kids, fishing off the jetti, Hannah's coffee caravan looking over the water & lots of bikes, kayaks and karts to hire. The staff are fantastic and very friendly. It's a 3 minute drive into Huskisson and centrally located to visit lots of other places in the area. We're booking to go back again in a few months. We can't wait.
Great place to stay modern spacious accommodation friendly professional staff and good amenities

This is our second visit to this park and definitely will not be the last. All sites are spacious. Yes it is a small park but it seems to work well due to layout. Yes we have our favourite site which fits our 20ft Van excellently. Some of our neighbours had 24ft Vans with still plenty of room left over. Staff friendly and helpful. Amenities clean and serviced regularly. Water pressure in showers almost drowns you. And it’s HOT! Great spot. We’ll be back definitely. Do yourself a favour.
Exotic feeling, good for families and friends. It is also really convenient for road trip travelers!

The team, Lea, Sarah, Aaron, Josh & Christina are all welcoming, friendly & kind. The park has a light airy vibe. The rooms are immaculate & super clean. The service from the team is second to none. I’ll be back. Thanks again for a pleasant & memorable stay & the recommendations.

장점 : 저비스베이를 둘러보기에 가까운 위치. 캠핑장을 지나 흐르는 강의 아름다움. 월삭이라 밤에 은하수를 볼 수 있었음. 4인용 캐빈은 조금 작지만 필요한 것들이 잘 갖춰져 있음. 수영장에 작은 미끄럼틀이 있고 얕은 곳부터 2미터 깊이까지 있어 재밌게 놀만함.
단점 : 많은 리뷰에서 지적했듯이, 체크아웃 할 때 베개 커버와 침대 시트, 이불 커버를 다 벗겨내야 하는 것이 귀찮음. 파크 측에서는 이게 우리의 규정이라고 하겠지만, 일반적인 행태는 아니라고 봄. 세탁기와 건조기를 사용하려면 1달러짜리 코인을 각각 5개씩 넣어야 하며, 세탁기와 건조기의 상태는 그다지 사용하고 싶지 않은 상태임.

Pros:
​Perfect Location: Great base for exploring the Jervis Bay area.
​Natural Beauty: The river flowing past the campground is stunning. Since we visited during a new moon, we were lucky enough to see the Milky Way at night.
​Well-Equipped Cabin: Although the 4-person cabin was a bit cozy (small), it had everything we needed.
​Fun Pool: The swimming pool has a small slide and ranges from shallow to 2 meters deep, making it fun for everyone.
​Cons:
​Inconvenient Check-out Policy: As mentioned in many other reviews, having to strip all the pillowcases, bed sheets, and duvet covers ourselves at check-out was quite a hassle. While the park claims it's "their policy," it’s certainly not standard practice for most paid accommodations.
​Laundry Facilities: You need five $1 coins each for the washer and dryer ($10 total). However, the machines were in such poor condition that I didn't really feel like using them.

Had the greatest stay in a 1 bedroom cabin here! Park is approximately easy 2km walk into town and just far enough away from town. The staff were so welcoming. The grounds are spotless & so well kept. The cabin was spotless. Will definitely be staying again. And if you have a boat, perfect place to launch it.
Was a good getaway with the family, Place is verry clean and well maintained.

Great experience — we loved the safari tent! It’s located right next to the swimming pool, which made it especially convenient with kids. Everything was spotless, and the tent had everything we needed for a comfortable stay. It’s also very close to town, making it easy to head out for food.||The only downside was that you’re expected to do part of the housekeeping before you leave. The reason given was health and safety, which didn’t really make sense to us — when you stay in a hotel, you’re not asked to strip the beds and gather the towels. It felt more like a cost-saving measure than a necessity. Overall, it seemed a little overpriced.

A++ Excellent. Weekend away with Family and New Friends was made all the better with the facilities and location.
The room was exceptionally clean, and well equipped.
Keep up the good work,  we will be back.

I will be honest I have not quite stayed here yet, but I had a family emergency and the staff were very understanding and helpful so I am already impressed and can’t wait to have a great weekend here soon.

Its a holiday park! Probably busy with families during summer break, less so on shoulder seasons.

We stayed in the Bushman's cabin (no ensuite).

Room was:
Clean
Comfortable
A/C worked great!

Shared bathrooms:
Clean,
Ample space (we stayed Sun-Tue)

Overall:
1km from town. Clean, safe and had all that we needed. Staff was kind and friendly. Two thumbs up! Budget friendly option.
Great place

Living in southern Sydney with visitors from overseas, we took a journey back in time - 50 years and visited the Shoalhaven area staying at the Tasman Holiday Park at Jervis Bay.|It turned out to be the best four days we have spent in many a year.  Not having a caravan or camper, we chose Cabin 26, a double bedroom with spa bath, located next to the boat ramp. Booking was easy online. The site was immaculately prepared, with well-maintained grounds, excellent cooking and showering facilities (for those not in vans or cabins). The pool was adjacent to the playground and like everything else - well maintained. Checking in was smooth and we easily found our cabin. One bonus of this park - no motorised bikes scooters etc. permitted - pedal power only. The park is also dog friendly.||Cabin 26 was surprisingly spacious for 3 adults and a 7yr old girl, who "claimed" the top bunk as hers. 😊 The few staff we encountered were very pleasant and jovial.|The fridge was full size, all cooking and cleaning utensils and linen provided and as a bonus an outdoor table overlooking Falls Creek.  The view from the balcony was magnificent. The boat ramp and jetty were a continual source of joy for our youngster as crabs, fish, ducks and pelicans were always on view. Though the highlight was watching the huge bull rays gliding by on most afternoons- stunning.||We also spent time snorkelling at Moona Moona Creek, swimming at the white sand Hyams Beach, eating magnificent pies from the Husky pie shop and visiting the Lady Denman Museum. The park was surprisingly busy – this being post school holiday time. The sunsets can be spectacular. See pics.||The entire experience was one of fun and relaxation.||One suggestion I would make it that a bench or seat be placed near the playground. It would make supervising kids much easier when you must stand for long periods of time. Oldies, pregnant mums and anyone really would benefit from somewhere to sit and watch the little ones.||As Arnie said..."I'll be back"

Zacznę od tego, że nie czytanie uważnie i zdawanie się na to, co wydaje mi się oczywiste, było w tym wypadku moim błędem, ale wpływa również na całość opinii. A mianowicie fakt wynajęcia pomieszczenia bez toalety. Coś, co w mojej opinii już nie występuje w cywilizowanym świecie. Fakt chodzenia 200 metrów przez kobietę w ciąży kilka razy w nocy do WC był bardzo kłopotliwy. Żeby uzasadnić fakt że obarczam tym bardziej hotel jest to, że próbowaliśmy dopłacić do pokoju z wc. Niezbyt miła obsługa (czego doświadczylismy już przy meldunku) powiedziała że nie ma nic wolnego. Chodząc w nocy widać było jednak kilka wolnych pokoi. Co spowodowało że nie mogliśmy ich dostać? Nie wiem. Pokoje klaustrofobiczne i sąsiedzi palący światło prosto w oczy przez całą noc dopełnili czary goryczy

Can’t believe the new management have let children be in the path of one of the only two ways to access your site.

The kids deserve to have an iconic ice cream, however, not at the expense of safety

We have stayed here twice on premium sites and love it. Love the location, so close to the creek, fishing and ramp for the boat, so close to the breweries, bakeries, large boat ramp and walks it's fantastic. The park is so well kept and deserves a 5 star rating. Smaller than most caravan parks, but the sites for vans / camping are spacious. Protected from the elements with beautiful fauna, large gum trees and mangroves, brings a host of wildlife to the waters edge like big stingray, water birds, and lots of fish. The staff are always tentive and friendly and amenities are clean and up to date. A short distance to town some 3km only and its a lovely walk. Bigger range of shops are available not far away in Nowra or closer by at Vincentia. The Bay itself and the views here are amazing and no doubt we will keep coming back. Great job Tasman Jervis Bay..!

Awesome location with good facilities.
Awesome staff and secure area as well.

They have free events during holidays wich is good!

Although the park is in a beautiful location and well maintained, it is disappointing that there are not clearer policies or better management practices in place to ensure all families can comfortably enjoy their stay.

During our visit, we were travelling with our baby and were placed directly next to a caravan occupied by heavy smokers. They also did not set up to the same direction of other caravans- horizontal to the road, they went vertical… they arrived later then others so you can see the way of the set up, but doing it this way unfortunately made their caravan back right of into our back area/ setting up right beside our outdoor set up- with their cigarette smoke blowing into our zone and caravan. It was too hot to close windows etc. Park policy also displays you must park a certain way (like the rest of us were) but the rules were not enforced by staff- resulting in this set of caravaners being practically in our space from the choice of set up direction….

When I raised my concerns with reception, I referred to the booking information, which states that there is “no smoking in accommodation or common areas, with designated smoking areas only.” This wording reasonably suggests that sites are smoke-free and that smoking is restricted to specific areas of the park, particularly as caravan sites are still classified as accommodation.

Unfortunately, our concerns were dismissed, and we were told that the neighbouring guests were permitted to smoke “outside” and that the park could not intervene. No alternative solutions were offered, which was disappointing given the circumstances and our travelling with an infant.

I suggested that, moving forward, the park consider asking guests at the time of booking whether they are smokers, allowing management to allocate sites more thoughtfully. You could EASILY have an area in the park for smoking set ups - as this would help create a healthier and more comfortable environment for families and non-smokers.
This suggestion was not taken seriously and was described as being too difficult or costly to manage, which I found surprising given that it would only simply involve better planning and no extra costs….

Considering the close proximity of sites and the nightly rate of $195 per site we paid- we expected a higher standard of customer service, consideration, and overall comfort. Unfortunately, this was not our experience, and it significantly impacted our stay.
I would also expect that trading under a large company name of ‘Tasman’ all caravan parks would have the same  policy for parks and standards.

They also advertise ‘coffee cart on site’ but due to being ‘low in staff’ they decided to use the barista as a house cleaner instead (asked reception why coffee was continuously not open and informed of the lack of staff/ their decision to accommodate her as an extra cleaner)  so it wasn’t open 3 of the days we stayed…. Which meant there was no where to grab a coffee and enjoy the nice scenery on the water that we payed so much to be there for WITHOUT having to drive into town…. I think staying open tell 10am to offer coffee as per paid advertisement for the park would have been more satisfactory for the people staying in the park THEN accomodating the staff as an extra cleaner/ help.

Speaking of staff- the general groundsman had a lot to say about staying guests to his former colleague in a very public/ commonly used place guests are. He had strong opinions that the guests currently staying are ‘disgustingly lazy’ and he is ‘sick of cleaning up after them’ sorry but that’s your job? Don’t like it- Get a new position/ job?

Honestly the overall experience was a massive disappointment

in der umgebung ist nicht viel los und der campingplatz ist recht teuer.
trotzdem ist es dort sehr schön, ruhig und sauber!

Stayed a few nights on a powered site. The sites are all turfed and absolutely huge! So nice to be so level and spacious, the whole holiday park is nicely laid out and not too busy. Facilities were modern and clean, really good.

We had a lovely time here! A huge thank you to Grant, who helped us out when a hose under our caravan sprang a leak, shortly after arriving! Super grateful! Kids loved the pool, slide, spa and playground area. The staff were attentive, prompt and helpful. The BBQ and bathroom facilities were clean and well maintained. The grounds had smooth roads/pavements making it perfect for bikes and scooters. There is also a footpath to connect the caravan park into town-about 30 minute walk. Being able to kayak in the creek using the park’s own jetty was a fantastic experience and very convenient. The kids really enjoyed this as well as the peddle carts. Lovely location and relaxing atmosphere. Looking forward to visiting again next year!
Beautiful, scenic, good crowd
Schöner, sauberer Campingplatz direkt am Creek gelegen. Das Sea Dog Café, welches auf dem Grundstück ist,war mein Highlight.
My fam had a great time there .

During our stay there was a large group of entitled people that had absolutely no respect for other park guests and management did absolutely nothing to ensure they abided by park rules, the worst weekend away I and other guests have experienced, will not be back..
Wow, such a beautiful  place  :-)

Absolutely beautiful stay!
We stayed in a waterfront cabin at Tasman Holiday Park Jervis Bay and the view was absolutely stunning. The villa was spotless — so clean, modern, and comfortable. The whole park is beautifully maintained, tidy, and peaceful. The staff were so friendly and welcoming, making us feel right at home. The location couldn’t be more perfect — right by the water and close to everything Jervis Bay has to offer. We can’t wait to come back!
Paid for the extra to have a spa & couldnt even use it ....it was filfy & discusting so dirty.
So neat, clean, organised, friendly. Great cafe on site.
Very clean, well laid out, excellent staff.|You could see the extra care taken to present clean and comfortable cabins.  Good job.
Lovely caravan park, however the kitchen and laundry being in the same tiny room was a downside.
The Deluxe Waterfront cabin is really good. We enjoyed the bath, bedroom, good view of living room and also the swimming pool. Very tidy and comfortable.🏡🚤🛶

Stayed 3 nights in our van on way to Victoria. Booking was easy, friendly service on arrival, our site was great, large astro turf, water & power close. A small park but beautifully set out and super clean. Plenty of cabins which looked fairly new. Hubby loved the pool and made good use of it during our stay. Close to Husskison which has everything you would need. Only one minus - only one bin right up front of reception.
Great place to stay

Beautiful camp site right on the river and close enough to town. The only downside is the amenities, 1 small fridge for the whole site is nuts, it's a shame something that could be rectified very easily is constantly mentioned in other reviews.
Pretty good campsite. The amenities were a little small for the amount of people there, had to wait to use them a few times but they were decent enough!

These are two of the most important topics when looking for somewhere to stay. ||The ease at which to book a site filled me with confidence no messy forms just straight and concise. ||The park itself is extremely well organised and extremely clean. ||We are a large setup, which we made clear when booking, the site was amazing. ||All the interactions we had were very helpful and straight to the point. ||Being only a few kms out of Huskisson made for a very easy walk or drive. ||What a great park and if ever we are back this way, will be sure to book again.
Had a great stay, check-in was easy, sites were huge, amenities clean, showers amazing. 20 min walk into Huskisson or 5 minutes to a local brewery.  Nice spot.

Amazing caravan park wow everything is immaculate and well maintained, the sites are excellent and cater really well for caravans and cabins alike. The staff are super friendly and it's not too far from town but you get the peace and quiet to enjoy a break.
Was Great Value For Money, The Location Was Awesome And Our Villa  Was Spacious Thankyou GODBLESS From The Hamill Family ❤️🙏🙏😊

We stayed in 2x ensuite site, they were very clean. Staff were very helpful and friendly.
Recommend for family with young children.
Close to lots of interest place. 30 minutes walk/ 5 minutes drive to the main shopping

We stayed in one of the onsite tents. Bad bad bad idea
Could not fit my car in parking space provided.
Next small children riding bikes around the car while I was trying to park in. We told them 3 times to please stop until we parked but to no avail.
Then the tent itself.
No lock of any kind on door so did not feel safe leaving out goods in tent while exploring the areas, and what was worse no lock to feel safe sleeping at night.
Then we were asked to strip the bed before we left in morning.
The bathroom door would not slide across so no privacy to use the bathroom.
The park was nice looking. But that was it. Could have stayed in 4 star motel in Hiskisson for less.
Also the tent was white so all lights in night came straight in the tent and all noise.

The Jervis Bay holiday park was such a great holiday experience for our son’s 40th birthday celebration, ! easy check in ,  it is so well set out, all safe for the kids , riding their bikes /scooters & central to Huskisson, & the local breweries. Thank you ,   The Whittakers

Nice place, our site was flat and all had astroturf. Good location and close to town. Safe site, we had bikes and e-skateboards and I wasn't worried all weekend. The only issue I had all weekend was the soap ran out in the toilet. I will be back, great place.
We booked the safari tent and loved it. Amazed as we had a torrential downpour for 24 hours and felt safe and warm.
Wonderful stay, great location! Would go again

The location is excellent and the reception is friendly Good options for those travelling with childrenThe kitchen could be better equipped, at least with the minimum to make something basic, and with more than one cooker There needs to be clear signposting of the accommodation (a couple entered our safari tent during the night thinking it was the place they had booked, we were a bit scared as it was late at night, but we managed to help them find where they were going to spend the night)
Very clean, nice gardens pool and playground

The Best Holiday Park I’ve stayed in!!!
Spotless grounds, large sites, boat parking, private boat ramp, pool and great amenities!
I would highly recommend staying here!

This was a lovely holiday venue to accommodate, we were intially going to book the Safari Tents of our family of five.
I wouldn't recommend the tents for large families considering there is no secure front door, and lack of privacy in the venue.
We decided on the Premium Garden Villa for two nights, it was a dream. Supplied with thick blankets, spacious rooms with wardrobe storage, useful cleaning supplies such as a broom and dust pan. Plenty of kitchenware with up to date essentials. AC was abit dated with a hard to read remote, we required assistance from one of the specialists at the front desk which is relatively close to the Villas to provide us a portable heater.

The Venue are accompanied a large BBQ area with benches and four barbecues- TV mounted on the wall. There is a large play-park area near the Villas near the entrance for big families with children! It's very spacious in open areas, however not many major beach locations near the venue- mostly consisting of water and damp sand depending on tides and transport.

The venue is located in a forested area, whilst it's reasonably close to a major Muesuem and Huskisson where you will need to drive- all of the shops providing food, gifts and docks are located there, including Whale and Dolphin Cruises.

Jervis Bay holiday Park was pretty nice. Our cabin had a great view onto the river. There was a pool which was super cold and the spa was also cold. The playground was nice but the slide was very slow. The spa in the cabin was really nice but the shower was slippery. Room was pretty nice just your average cabin. There was also kids buggys but they were a little pricey. Location wasn't the greatest but it was ok. About 5 mins away from main town. Overall pretty good caravan park.
Beautiful location great facilities close to shops but not too close Would highly recommend and would stay again

Really lovely small-ish quiet caravan park by the river. About 2km easy walk to Huskisson shops. Great water pressure in the shower, Only downside, not much space to put clothes etc, but that certainly wouldn't put me off returning here.

A great dog friendly park near Huskisson.  Always a great place to stay. The manager is  helpful and friendly. While you are there visit the nearby Kraken Sourdough Bakery. Your stomach will thank me.
친구랑 웜뱃 보러 왔다가 묵었는데 두 번째 방문할 정도로 너무 좋았습니다, 위치도 좋고 요리하기도 좋지만 화장실이 밖에 있어서 아쉽고 소금은 챙겨가세요.
Great stay. Lovely staff and nice sites. Hot plate wasn't working in kitchen which was a shame but not the end of the world
A lot of fun games to play and two parks!
Only 5 showers and 5 toilets for camping patrons.

Off peak so mice & quiet. Love having the shade cloth under our feet. Caravan floors stayed clean the 3 nights we were there. Lovely sitting down the river where tables are provided with a lovely grassed area.

Our experience was quite disappointing. We were under the impression that our booking was for the entire 4-bedroom villa, as we paid $650. However, we were informed that charges were incurred per person, resulting in additional fees for 6 adults and 2 kids. The staff were extremely rude throughout our stay.

I specifically requested a full river view for the villa during our phone call, but upon arrival, we found it only had a partial view. Additionally, despite the website stating that the park is pet-friendly, the staff made a comparison to Hilton, suggesting that one wouldn't take a pet to a Hilton property because not all villas allow pets. This is information that should have been communicated during our phone conversation,  it resulted in an extra $100 cleaning fee.

Overall, this experience marred the joy of our family gathering, and I cannot recommend this place.
The park is very clean the staff very friendly and helpful and welcoming. Our villa overlooking the water was wonderful situated close to town and many other places would definitely come back .

Awesome place to stay. The caravan park is meticulously clean and tidy and the staff were welcoming. Stayed with two pooches and a short walk in to town. Very relaxing. (No rating on the rooms as I stayed on a powered caravan site).
We had a lovely one night stay here, amazing, quiet place to stop by and enjoy picturesque area.

We were absolutely spoilt here. A beautiful caravan park with nice amenities. Little touches like small lights along pathways to see where you’re going etc. Comfortable showers, clean kitchen and bbq area. Definitely staying here again.
Thay are sting rays

We loved our stay at Jervis Bay Holiday Park - amazing friendly reception team, park was immaculate -well set out with pool, playground, well set up camp kitchen, cabins were spotless and in very good condition - direct access to river and jetty’s for  fishing!! Even had a coffee van providing coffee etc for guests - well done to Jervis Bay Holiday Park Team - we will definitely return !!!
This is a top place for holidays with Family. Look after the place as the people in charge are quality
Wonderful park, very clean and well maintained. Lovely staff, completely pet and child friendly. Only issue was the pool was freezing. Would definitely come back and stay. Plus a nice walk into town.
Amazing place to stay. Good. Clean. Affordable and near the city.
Great spot to stay away from the busy streets, but close to town and a couple of excellent breweries and bakery. Set on a quiet riverside location, perfect for paddleboarding. Great facilities too.

We were disappointed with the distance into town as we have a campervan and it wasn't really comfortable walking distance.  And also we were in amongst 4 families who were there together all with young children who were loud, and constantly at our site because we had a dog.  Parents were no where to be seen.

Great locations, fantastic service , friendly staff and management , spotlessly clean , lots of extra equipment available  for use in the park !
Loved it and will return !

Great park. Very clean and superb friendly service. Excellent location on the river and nice extras such as coffee van , live music and burger van. Our stay was tainted by a group that was self centred, loud and drunk . No supervision of their kids who mostly had better manners than the parents. Had to listen to their antics till 12.30am. The park policy of zero tolerance to noise means nothing if not enforced. There is an intercom at reception for emergencies but no mobile number to report noise. We will return but will avoid weekends.

Was beautiful  will be back again
Dog friendly

Such a gem, located close enough to Huskisson via walking track/bike or even kayak down via the river.

The caravan park is small, which is our favourite part. They don't have people partying all through the night. However, still have a great atmosphere with the fire pit, communal bbq area, and waterfront gathering spot.

We love to fish off their private warf and just walk back to the van when done, No driving.

They also have their own small boat ramp, which people use when hiring or bringing their own water toys.

The pool and spa are not heated. However, I have never had a cold shower yet, which kind of makes up for it.

They do have an onsite caravan that serves coffee tea, etc, over the weekends and busy periods.

We have stayed in the large family cabin, but prefer to take our van down as we love to take our dogs.

We love to ride to the two local breweries, great food vans wine beer, and love music. This is also dog friendly. You can easily walk as it's only 10 mins down the rd.

The staff are so helpful, and honestly, this is my favourite getaway....

ENJOY
Good location on river. Very nice tidy clean park. Can walk into huskisson in 30min if your keen.

One of the best caravan parks on the south Coast.
Only easy system, very clean.
I like this synthetic grass. In this environment, it's a lot better than real grass.
Its easy to find and there is a great couple of breweries up the road.
And polite and friendly staff top it off
Staff are absolutely wonderful and very helpful. Well maintained and clean. My toddler and I have had a wonderful stay. Thank you!
Very enjoyable stay!

Jervis Bay Holiday Park is as good as parks get, we have visited there a hand few of times over the past few years. The grounds and facilities are outstanding, the location is incredible and the owners & staff are an absolute pleasure to deal with.  See you next time guys! Thanks Blake
We were in our van on this trip. The park was so good set out of the park was great.
Would definitely stay here again
Great park and clean amenities lots to see and great hospitality. Boat ramp and jetty in the park highly recommend 👌

Terrible amenities. 2 toilets and 2showers down the bottom end. My grey water had to run uphill so I had to hold the hose up to empty it.  Only one place for the rubbish.  Skip bins at the front of the park.  The sites themselves were fabulous.  Very large with astroturf.   The park has its own jetty and boat ramp.  If they fixed the rubbish problem and the amenities/ camp kitchen,  I would have given them 5 stars.

When we got there I had already booked site 6 which was at the front and a large site as we had two dogs so we were out of the way. Mistake apparently everyone thought it was ok to walk through our site to get to the water front even though there was a paved walkway to the front. One lady with her little fluffy dog moved our chair and stepped over our ropes to get through. My husband said “your walking through out site with your dog, good job ours are in the caravan” . She replied” this is a walk though and I can walk through with my dog”. Rude as. If my dogs were outside, and tied up like they are suppose to be they would have gone mad. This happened at least one a day.  Now the non powered sites are really closed together not spread out like on the park map.  We had a couple with their two kids and dog arrive, thankfully on our last night,  the kid was screaming most of the time while they were putting up the tent and then, and thank got my dogs were again inside, his dog on a lead but no tied up walks into our site. Oh sorry the man says. Honestly not good enough. So if you book site 6 note that site A is right on your doorstep and apparently it’s ok for your kids to ride their bikes through your site. Nightmare.  Also there are only two toilets and two showers(ladies) in one amenity block for a  whole lot of people.

We would have been better off in the middle of the park on a site.

It was great sitting by the water at night but that’s about it.

The location was amazing and swimming pool + facilities were well maintained. Great place to stay for family trip especially if you have young children. Close to the shops and restaurants nearby. One thing to be aware though, I booked on trip.com making sure it’s booked for the right number of people (i.e. 1 adult + 3 children). At checking in, I was advised to pay extra for children as the rate did not include them, instead it was the rate for 2 adults. As I understood that it was not holiday park’s fault so I agrees to pay; but then I had to pay for 3 children even though there was only 1 adult. It was a long 3.5 hr drive with heavy rain, all I wanted was to check in and rest so paid extra AUD45.
Good caravan sites
Great park, great location. Amenities always clean BUT there's only 3 toilets and showers for both mens and ladies, not enough.
Wonderful place
This really is a 3 starplace.

This is a great park. Clean and In a good spot. The only thing that was most annoying was when people walked straight through your site without even giving it a thought. Why don't caravan parks make it one of their policy to not walk through other sites.

We camped one night as part of a road trip, and the park is a beautiful stop. Great layout and large pet friendly camp site. Awesome setting by the water to sit and watch the sunset. Would stay again.

Great location to local super market, club and pub within 5mins. 10-15 mins to Coles and Woolworth.

It has shared kitchen, BBQ, shower, toilet, also food truck from 8am or 8:30am based weekday or weekend. Pool area not very big , has one simple water slide, it also have SPA beside.
Nice facilities and location
Nice accomodation and nice with a pool, that our kids enjoyed!
Clean and modern amenities. Spacious sites. Friendly staff
Its nice and peaceful place to relax.
Great place to stay, great accommodation to choose from, great staff, and great activities for the kids, we will definitely be coming back to this place.

We had a powered site which was large, close to amenities, the creek and food van, a pleasant weekend despite the consistent rain.
Staff were friendly and helpful.

What an amazing holiday park. My daughter and I spent 3days in the Safari Tent this week. Our tent had aircon, kitchen and a bathroom. The park was beautiful and the service was amazing. Thank you again. 10/10 😊

Stand out park for us and we have stayed in many!
Nice small park, great facilities, amazing location and such a great feel.
Loved every minute, wish we could have stayed longer
Had a lovely stay here with family, accommodation was great, friendly staff and close to shops
My campervan had a great position. Loved the little on-site coffee van. Friendly ataff and beautiful location
I’m impressed

Sorry guys but criticism is to be done! I was here 5 years ago and nearly every thing changed here since then - not everything to the better!!! The sites, the drive ways 1st class! The amenities are completely new too but they are a catastrophe! Too small, too less and they are stinking like hell!!!! If you go for a shower you can choose between a shower directly beside a toilet or beside the urinal!!! Who wants this choice?
Who planned this amenities? He/she should be jailed or sentenced to use this amenities daily 5 times.
Staff really friendly and helpful - very, very polite!
Fees not too expensive - 300 $ 2 adults, powered site, 5 nights.
Fantastic place to stay 😄 park has three dog friendly cozy cabins so I can take my dog 😁
Small place nothing much for the kids

Beautiful park and close to everything. The park is amazingly maintained, great coffee van and activities. The river is great to paddle board on and boat access is easy with the ramp. The perfect spot for a huge family gathering, we had van sites and cabins. Highly recommend!

Nice park, well maintained, compact little ensuites for the van, unreal showers, nice pool area, coffee van with burger nights on Fridays, plenty of concrete area for the kiddos, astro turf everywhere - no mud! Very clean, will be back.

Stayed at a newer cabin with front water view which came with a bbq stove at the patio. The bbq stove is quite convenient. Short drive to shops and attractions.
Downsides:
Have to remove the linens before check out and clear out the garbage. Wouldnt expect to do all that with the price we are paying.
Beware of the cockroaches, maybe that's why they have an insect spay in every cabin. Can't say it's quiet if you are staying at the cabin near the boat ramp.

I find it bizarre that a place that charges near hotel rates for a cabin can be so precious as to require me to strip the bed of linen, and take the garbage out to the bins. The reason given for this work was to protect their staff from Covid (an airborne disease). They also had two signs mentioning fees for not leaving the cabin in a reasonable condition - one at $50 and one at $100. I also do not appreciate text messages reminding me to carry out the tasks, and then asking for reviews. I am sure you could find somewhere better to stay.
Well-maintained campsite with enough activities to keep the kids entertained. Also located quite near to the Jervis Bay attractions.
Caravan park was exceptionally clean. Location beautiful

Nous étions en camping tantes, trop jolie vue sur la rivière, calme et paisible. L’accueil était tout simplement parfait. Est parti commune était propre (sanitaire, douche) Nous n’avons pas eu le temps de l’utiliser, mais il y a un petit brasero a disposition. Vraiment très bonne expérience.
Awesome quiet lovely

Great place to stay, close walk to town.
The staff/owners all were very friendly and helpful. Very clean amenities, camp kitchen &  laundry.  Great place for families. Pet friendly. Coffee Van.
Best campground ever! So safe, clean and staff are amazing ~ would highly recommend for any time of the year :)
Beautiful spot, well maintained m, super friendly staff and amazing locations. Plenty of things to do for the whole family . highly recommend!!
Very interesting place with many facilities. Stunning location

Once again, we have thoroughly enjoyed our stay at JBHP. Best holiday park we've stayed at and that's why we keep coming back!! Great caravan sites that are roomy, flat and have artificial grass. Fantastic amenities. Very clean and tidy park, the owners take great care with it and all staff are wonderful to deal with. Even has a coffee/breaky food van on some days!! Great fire pit to mingle with other campers. Love it!
Very good place to stay
整體燈光不夠，暖氣到零晨完全不夠，凍醒了幾次！！設施可以，小朋友很喜歡！如果有多幾張毛毯 應該不會凍醒！
Beautiful Holiday Park. Loved it.
Very clean park with great amenities. Beautiful coastal feeling will be back
Powered site,water view, perfect great park
Wonderful 😊
the park has changed so much over the years and it looks amazing

Camped for 6 nights on an ensuite site, ensuite was clean, hot shower and appreciated the dual rainfall shower head and  hand held hose. Camp ground was compacted which did not allow for pegs but was provided with a drill and screws upon check in which solved the problem and the tent held securely through wind and rain. Campsite itself is very orderly and well planned with plenary of room for parking. Only negative was noise from the road early morning which cannot be helped.

We booked a water front villa in April and our family loved it. It was beautiful and quiet. The cabin was clean and spacious. It had clean sheets and towels. The view was amazing. You could fish, rent kayaks and paddle bike, swim in the pool or just enjoy the view from the cabin. The owner was friendly and accommodating. We would definitely come back.

Great place to stay, close to town and the breweries. Nice fire, bathrooms were clean and smelt great, tidy and clean as well as a coffee van and music on the Friday night. The caretaker of the park was very helpful with anything we needed. Great place to stay!

We stayed here for 2 nights in a little cabin as the weather was too bad for camping. The shared amenities were spotless (the cleanest I’ve seen in a campground) and the cabin was really nice. Staff is very helpful and friendly. This is not your nature/middle of nowhere campground but still great.
Payed for two nights and only stayed one kept up till 1:30am from people having  a party next door

Wow, what an awesome place to holiday. With top shelf cabins, clean & modern. If I had a caravan I would definitely stay here & with their Astroturf there is no dirt or sand getting into your van. Beautiful pool, spa, BBQ area & kids play ground will keep everyone happy, not to mention the onsite boat ramp & jetty. The staff Helen, Scott & Mark are so friendly & have done an amazing job building this park up over the last 20 years. I hope they are there for another 20.

Great place for a quick little R&R trip . The holiday park was very accommodating and the staff were helpful . Cabins were on the small side but clean and tidy . A lovely dog friendly area with lots to do .

What an absolute cracker of a park! Perfect for a ‘civilised’ stop to get caught up on all the things that slip while offgrid!
The turf sites are level, getting in and out was an absolute breeze (highly recommend site 19 for size AND close proximity to the pool and amenities!).
The location was perfect for exploring and the coffee van in the morning and icecream van in the afternoon meant we were all well happy! Plus a couple of breweries walking distance down the road is always a plus!

Wir haben insgesamt 3 Nächte auf dem Camping gebucht. Die Lage ist sehr gut. Die Waschräume sind einfach aber ausreichend. Unsere Beschwerden sind die Folgenden: Am zweiten Abend stand ein Boot mit Auto quer auf unserem Platz. Da wir spät ankamen, konnten wir die Person nicht finden und vom Servicepersonal hat sich vorher anscheinend auch niemand darum gekümmert. Schade.. Leider waren an 2 Nächten Gruppen auf dem Campingplatz sehr laut. Bei einer anderen Rezension habe ich gelesen, dass Sicherheitspersonal für Ruhe sorgen würde. Ich weiss nicht wo dieses Sicherheitspersonal war, auf dem Camping auf jeden Fall nicht. Am 23.04. schrien bis nach 24 Uhr Betrunkene herum. Als andere Gäste sie schliesslich um Ruhe baten, pöbelten sie diese auch noch an. Als Gast auf einem nicht gerade billigen Platz finde ich dies inakzeptabel und bitte darum die Sicherheitskontrollen ernst zu nehmen!

We enjoyed a 2 week stay at the Jervis Bay Holiday Park, even during school holidays it wasn’t terrible. Kids can be very loud and zoom around everywhere on various modes of travel but that’s unavoidable at almost every park. Owners put on entertainment for everyone with a Bug and critter show which was very popular and musicians also played a few times. There is a coffee van right next to the boat ramp, pool, and surprisingly the entire park is either concrete, bitumen, artificial turf, and a type of tight woven matting, this makes it very clean and rain water clears quickly. There is a community fire pit and no personal fires for obvious reasons, the amenities block was on the small side, only 2 showers, 2 toilets, a urinal and 2 hand basins, all were clean. It took 5 minutes to Huskisson in car, 10 min on push bike and about 30 on foot, also just 500m from the park are a few great shops in the industrial park, check out the beautiful muscles and oysters at the Jervis Bay shellfish, the bakery and the 2 craft brewery’s which also do food. Plenty of things to do like  walking tracks, cycle tracks, lovely beaches, water activities, sightseeing cruises, dolphins and whale cruises, diving, surfing and not far to the mountains and Sydney.

Disgusting experience!! Save yourself the trouble and find a different spot to crash.

The rooms were straight up filthy, like they haven't seen a cleaning crew in ages. And don't even get me started on the staff, they were beyond rude. It's like they forgot that their job is to provide good service. I wouldn't recommend that place to my worst enemy.
Zeer strakke en moderne camping. Alles is goed onderhouden en de locatie aan een meer is geweldig! Goede douches en een fijn zwembad, al zou het fijn zijn als deze verwarmd kan zijn
Would love to go back again. You could hire kayaks and tricycles from the park Itself. Next to the river, at night i had the chance to observe bioluminescence on soil at night when the tide was low.
Great stay last month.

We have done our fair share of trips in our van and this is up there with one of the best spots we have been the sites are fantastic very spacious and clean. It’s small enough to let the kids ride around and know they are safe let’s just say our kids didn’t want to leave!! You know a good park when you re book when your there and that’s what we did 😊

We had the experience of staying at the Jervis Bay Holiday Park over the weekend! It was an amazing time shared between family. However, as we entered the our cabin which was a great deal of money, I noticed cockroach’s in the kitchen area. Which at first we thought it’s okay. However, at night time as I came to sleep in the bunk room, we saw a cockroach in a hole right where the pillow is placed.

Being on a campsite it is understandable to see insects etc, but inside a brand new cabin it is not something you expect especially when paying over 2k. That night I ended up sleeping on the couch as I could not bear the thought of cockroaches crawling in the beds!

The next morning my cousin approached the reception and told them about the issue, however this seemed to be brushed off and we were given a can of inspect spray. It’s disappointing as this is not a guests job to be pest controlling whilst on holiday.

Additionally, multiple of our family members who stayed next door also encountered cockroaches inside the bedside drawers and in the kitchen area!

I recommend for the owners to really remove mattresses off the bunks and give it a good pest control and keep it maintained and also have the cleaners really give it a good clean as this is not something a guest wants to experience whilst on holiday. As much as I enjoyed my time at the camp time and loved every moment, the cockroach issue in the cabins and beds will definitely be keeping me away from coming back unfortunately!
This place is so beautiful, its got all you need here

Buyer Beware
The cabins were fine as was the location although it is not near the beach.
At $191.00 for a garden view villa on a midweek night out of school holidays it is certainly at the high end of the pricing chain. When booking over the phone no mention was made of a Senior's discount only being available for 2 nights or more. It was only pointed out when we asked on arrival that it was on the website.
Not negotiable.
For the high price and the lack of disclosure it is a bit cheeky asking that all linen and towels be stripped and placed into linen bags for the cleaners.
The park is clean however the poor experience with the cost and duties required, means that we will not be back.

Thank you to all the staff 🙏 💙 ❤️
They allowed me to have my fur babies wile seeing my elderly mum .
This is a special time for me to spend this time with my mum and wouldn't be able to without this place.
This is my second time here and I wouldn't be able to do this without the the amazing staff ❤️.
I would highly recommend this place to and family friends or anyone that wants a time out .
They have pool BBQ facilities everything is so clean 😍.

Highly recommend  5 🌟 🤩

The staff here made this possible for something that money can't replace

God bless you this place 🙏 and anyone who stays here .

Lovely 😍 amazing awesome and more thN words can say.

Thanks once again
Great park near Huskisson

We spent 4 nights at this park. Service was exceptional we were offered an upgraded site without asking. Amenities were very clean, in fact whole park was very well looked after. Wasn’t far from town and great accesses to waterways. Park felt very safe. Coffee van opened from 8.00am to 10.00 am very nice touch and very good meeting place, very good coffee too.
We highly recommend this park and we will definitely visit again.

We stayed in a waterfront cabin which had an amazing view and was a tranquil stay however there were a few things that let us down with this caravan park. One of them is that we encountered multiple cockroaches in our accomodation which left us feeling like the cabin wasn't well cleaned. We also feel it's completely unreasonable to ask guests to strip their own bedding and take out their bins and even the cheapest accomodation we have stayed in has never asked this of us. Thirdly the bed was sooo squeaky and the mattress springy and uncomfortable! This needs a serious update. This could have easily been a 5 star stay for us but unfortunately won't be back.

This is a beautiful park, clean, neat with great facilities. Staff are wonderful to deal with helpful to the extreme. My only complaint is night time noise monitoring. Our first two nights had party groups nearby  until after 11.00. The unpowered section is much quieter.

Wir waren 4 Nächte dort, der CP ist ruhig gelegen die Plätze ohne Strom groß, es wäre perfekt wenn noch Wasserhähne dort vorhanden wären. Die sanitären Anlagen waren sehr sauber 👍. Wir würden auf jeden Fall wieder kommen.
Very basic

Been here several times and as leaving yesterday I popped in to the office to book again. Staff always attentive and friendly. The whole park we have always found to be kept pristine and is laid out very well. Roads within the whole park are generously sized making RV/caravan parking much easier than many other caravan parks. There is also a specific area to park your boat which also has a large vehicle turning area. The amenities block is centrally located in the park with a laundry, washing lines, public fridge/freezer, showers and of course toilets. All kept exceptionally clean. The pool area has adequate sun loungers with overhead sun cover. There is a working spa adjacent to the pool and a small young children’s playground also close to the pool area. The rubbish bins area is at the entrance/exit in an enclosed area for all to discard rubbish. It too is kept clean and never have we experienced any odours in that area which is away from all caravans, cabins, tents. Some other parks we have been to the bins are scattered throughout the park and often they smell. Not here. Also, you can easily ride your bike or walk into Huskinson from the park or go to the two breweries across the road. Do rate Jervis Bay Holiday Park? You bet I do. And, no I do not work for them or have any family and friends that do either. We just love it.
What a great holiday park. So close to everything.  Will definitely book again.
Beautifully presented, clean pool located on the river.

Great place to visit with kids. You have to clean the apartment and pack the used linen in the given bags and empty bins before departure. Legs of furniture and exhaust fans were bit dusty. But great place and my kids didn't wanna leave. We enjoyed our stay.

Would have been 5* but the ensuite sites have extremely hard surface which needs hammer drill to get the pegs in - not ideal if you have strong winds and rain. Had to turn around and drive to JB to get el drill - even with that it wasn't enough. All campers had the same issue. Credit to the staff - they tried to help.
Great park for kids and for their parents. Worth the visit!
Friendly staff, great location & holiday activities were a plus

It's not really by the beach, but definitely by the waterfront.
- It does get crowded and busy/noisy in the day, but definitely quiet at night time.
- Rooms (we got a pet friendly cabin) are basic but clean, and definitely functional.
- Bummer is that it is not walking distance to Huskisson so you do have to plan to drive back if you're having dinner in town. There is a food truck, which we did not try. There was live entertainment on the Wednesday night.
- Another bummer with location is that it isn't walking distance to Huskisson beach, which is leash-free for dogs after 4pm in summer -- it's an easy drive, but still a drive.
Beautiful
Amazing place...fully recommend..

Great location and very clean campsite. The only problem we had was that the tent sites, with ensuites, were on such hard compacted ground that it was almost impossible to hammer the pegs in. Several other campers had the same problem as well. In the end we couldn't be bothered to set up the marquee as well because of this, hence no shade.

The experience and amenities were great! We had a camp site with an ensuite which was not suitable for camping. The site was on compressed sand with a shadecloth material on the ground so it was impossible to put in tent pegs. We even went and bought expensive heavy duty pegs were unable to get them in the ground.  We spoke to the staff who just said lots of people had said the same thing. It made the experience a bit difficult. Otherwise the place was great.
The best place to stay if you are visiting jervis bay.

Just had 4 nights on a powered site. We were here 5 years ago, and can't believe the difference.  There has been a huge amount of money spent. Artificial grass, swimming pool, coffee van, amenities spotless. Definitely will be back. Thanks.

Lisa

Prior to checking in I called to advise that we may be checking in a bit later in the day so I can confirm what their procedure was. During the conversation I was asked who is staying there and I replied two adults and 2 children. I was told each child would be an extra $15 a night which I disputed as there was a queen sized bed and 4 bunk beds in the 2 bedroom unit and Expedia does not advise of this charge and we are not over the maximum amount of guests. After a formal complaint with them they dropped the fee. To add a further sour taste in our mouth there was signage in the unit and also messages sent they we were expected to strip all the bedding and towels and ensure all washing up is done, take all our rubbish out to the front of the complex or we would be charged a $100 cleaning fee. Whilst I'm okay with washing up a few plates etc. I believe it's completely unreasonable to expect guests when they are on holidays to strip all their own bedding etc. and its either lasiness or cost cutting in my opinion by the holiday park. Further the hot water was patchy where is would be extremely hot to nearly burn or too cold and not constant. The unit was also average in cleanliness and the window tracks not clean, curtains stained and lounges average. I find their pricing very expensive if you basically have to do some of their cleaning for them and unless the staff attitude changes with their unreasonable demands we won't be staying their again.
Excellent caravan park although some of the sites are a bit tight to get a van into. Great location and well kept.

We stayed in the dog friendly cabin.
Very small with virtually no kitchen bench.
There were signs everywhere saying “guests must clean up all dishes followed by threats of $100 cleaning fees”
Then guests must remove all linen and place in linen bags prior to leaving.
We normally leave our hotels/ cabins in accumulate condition but when your told to do it when paying be an absolute premium to stay somewhere it make me not want to do it.
You also were told to empty all rubbish and recycle then we were only given one rubbish bag for a 3 night stay.
It’s very artificial and well manicured so if your into real nature you won’t find that here.
The receptionist were friendly.
It’s extremely cramped and no window in our cabin was private every one we looked out was visible to someone.
So over all, it was not really our style.

Great location along the inlet. Very tranquil!
The grounds were very well maintained and the camping sites were well marked.

If you rely on a camp kitchen when camping, then please be prepared for only minimal bench space, fridge space, and very old appliances.

One things that dampened the stay was the other guests. Drunken loud conversations well into the night.

Stayed here a few times always clean, tidy and helpful, has a coffee cart
Close to a local brewery
Close to Husky for shops
River out the front for boats and fishing

We stayed for a few days with our van on the artificial grass site, we loved our stay, friendly staff & clean amenities although they could be a little larger as my clothes got wet while I was showering but otherwise was a great stay & will return but next time we will go with the ensuite site

Great location, with bus stop outside to shops and restaurants, otherwise half hour walk.
Bathrooms modern and very clean.
Kitchen is small with an electric hob, fridge, and microwave, only concern for me was the washing machine and dryers I were In the kitchen, and the thought of doing my food preparation where other people are washing bedding etc, feels unsanitary.
We stayed in our RTT, ground was level throughout the site.
An excellent spot for a family trip away. Large waterfront cabins with private access to a boat ramp/jetty in Woolamia creek. The park is very well maintained too.
Another good value caravan park , lovely sites , clean & well looked after amenities,  staff lovely.  Great spot

Not usually a fan of caravan parks at all. This place was actually quite nice and not a carpark masquerading as a refugee camp. Nicely spaced sites. A communal firepit with wood. Had a good time relaxing.
Awesome park that is tucked out the way.
Дякую, дуже сподобалося!

Tanya at the reception is AMAZING, will be back thanks to her !!! I definetly will be back because of the beautiful location and really good amenities for children. The riverside spots, bbq areas are convenient and tidy. Would recommend it without hesitation !
Our garden villa was in pristine condition. The surroundings are nice and well kept. And the neighbours are nice and friendly
First time here towing a van after a long trip from Perth.  What a lovely, relaxing location with clean facilities. Perfect for the kids and adults alike.  Definately coming back here next trip.
Clean and cozy 🤍
Lacking a good kitchen area but over all pretty good.

Lovely peaceful location. Clean amenities and kitchen. Friendly staff.
Lovely nature walk to beach and river/creek.  Nearby to other towns, shops and attractions.  Good size caravan sites.
Great fishing, great beaches &  great everything!
Not suitable for tent camping. Site is designed more for a caravan and not for tent. No grass. Kitchen is not equipped properly. No stove for cooking!

Facilities were surprisingly clean, almost spotless. I wasn’t sure what to expect based off some other reviews. Regarding people living on site, everyone was extremely friendly and it was a nice vibe. My only downside is that the unpowered camp sites really feel and look like you’re in the back end of the park and all campsites are in a row next to each other. Aesthetically it wasn’t the best but otherwise comfortable! The beach however is a quick and easy walk with plenty of beautiful wildlife to see.

While the park is price friendly it has 80% of accommodate as permanent cabins or holiday homes. This tends to lead to heaps of younger kids roaming the park very late making heaps of yelling and screaming. As they are all regular visitors or owners park officials don’t police this. Very hard to get young kids to sleep. This happens most nights. On a positive there was a great band in camp kitchen on news year eve.
Virkelig ikke en hyggelig camping. Undgå stedet

Wow! Kangaroos mingle with the guests after dusk, the Crimson Rosella’s too. A friendly environment with great facilities. A small walk to the beautiful sands of Myola, Jervis Bay. What more could you ask for.

Lovely cabin, though floor wasn't clean, blowfly on window sill on arrival, dirty paw mark left on interior sliding glass door on arrival, wasp nest under awning of cabin which was known to them after we brought it to their attention though it was overlooked.

The holiday park is very run down with decrepit looking caravans and bungalows. It has reasonable facilities and is close to the ferry and beach but if your looking for charming surroundings this is not it.

If you’re up for a good valued family holiday this spot is great.

The somewhat private beach access you get to some of the whitest and smooth sand you’ll ever see if well worth it.

While lacking in some services, the remote area makes up the difference.

Also, the pool was really well looked after and clean as well as the rooms.

We will be back 👌

One night stopover was not as expected from reviews or price.  Got a pet friendly cabin that smelled awful even tho windows were open on arrival.  Best thing was the brilliant white towels in the bathroom with wobbly toilet seat, spiders (?) and mouldy silicon.  Wouldn’t have minded so much if I could have detected some disinfectant but the musty pet smell was uber uncomfortable.

Un camping situé proche de Jervis bay, en bordure de forêt. De nombreux kangourous et oiseaux fréquentent le camping. Un soir j'ai failli rentrer en collision avec un kangourous qui broutait l'herbe à la sortie de mon van

Very clean and well stocked cabin, comfortable bed and pillows. Very friendly and helpful staff. Short walk to the beach, No road noise.
Thank you!
Great location!  A really beautiful spot.  Cleanliness was great, they really do look after the park.  Well done 👏
I definitely won't be coming back ! The place was absolutely filthy!

Our 4th time here twice in our caravan and twice in a water front villa. This trip wasn’t our best the rooms are very clean and tidy on the down side a horrible smell from the waste drain in toilet, the shower and very low pressure that’s if you could get the temperature right, the shower kept leaking the whole time, the tv in the bedroom could hardly get a Chanel. For the price of the room we didn’t expect we would have to work it (stripping the beds) for us we weren’t fans of the concrete and fake grass everywhere, but that’s just us. We liked it the old way.

We stayed at this park last month after being recommended by our daughter who had previously been there. It was a lovely park, next to a river with large,clean sites. What we loved about it was the ground was either concrete or artificial grass which meant no grass or mud in the van after rain. The toilets were lovely and clean and there were plenty of facilities to use.It was a leisurely half hour walk into Huskisson and not far from two breweries for a meal and a beer! We are planning on returning with the family next year and being only an hour from home, it's a good getaway destination for a few days relaxation. 10/10.
Great location, friendly park, helpful lovely staff. Perfect stop for us.

Short Q & A.
Amenities ✅Location ✅Management ✅People at Park❤️✅ Tv & phone 5 & 4G, excellent reception ✅. Dogs all on leashes✅👌. Peace & Tranquility👍❤️❤️✅.
Kids on bicycles, keep your eyes open, but kids will be kids,, so you can’t change that.
Overall We HIGHLY recommend this place.
Huge caravan lot spaces at a very competitive price for the area, particularly in that area.

If you're looking for a camping spot to reconnect with the nature or some sort of wilderness, this spot is NOT for you. Almost ideally manicured, with zero natural grass or soil (everything is covered by artificial grass and concrete). We were trying to find a stick for a marshmallow, and we literary couldn't. Some may say "it's neat", and I understand there is target audience for this, but if you're on a bit of "wild" side, you'll be disappointed. Another disappointment was the fire pit. Yes, it looks great on photos and nicely designed, but super crowded at night, forget "sit and relax with your family" type of fire time. On a good side - it's super clean, well-maintained and well-organised, friendly owners, great showers, pet friendly, great playground and a pool, coffee van for your morning coffee (don't expect any breakfast food, just coffee, muffins ans croissants, but the coffee is great).
Very new in this van park and the sites are very large and level ground the amenities are very clean

The room was spacious and clean when we arrived. All utensils are available in the kitchen.

Staff was friendly when we checked in. Kayak and fee outdoor activities for kids and adults are available.

There is a coffee available inside opens at 8am called Sea Dog.

It's few minutes drive to Huskissons Town Centre and walking fistance to Jervis Bay pub.

Nice place ,clean tidy amenities, friendly staff  only down fall is I got note on table in room stating  to strip sheets and pillowcases off bed and put in laundry bag provided isn't  that's what you employ cleaners for otherwise nice place
Beautiful,  clean, friendly,  plenty to do for kids. Loved our time here
Very neat and tidy holiday park. Close to town with boat ramp, jetty for fishing, canoe / paddle board hire, pool, facilities and great staff. Thankyou 🙂
Great experience and an awesome place to be in
Stayed in the Glamping tent was bigger than our friends Bushman's hut and had all the comforts
Second time here and it was as great as the first. Ppl are friendly facilities are well looked after, couldnt ask for more :)
Great memories created. Thanks to the very friendly team. Beautiful site. Beautiful beach minutes walk away.  We will be back. Thank you again. Very happy father and son experience

Only stayed one night. Was a bit far out of the way from things. Toilets and showers were clean although took a while for hot water to come through as I think I was the only one who used the showers that night. Only us and a big family group seemed to be there. Camp kitchen was basic with washing up sinks outside the toilets rather than in the kitchen. Could do with some plugs for those sinks. Fantastic friendly wildlife on site! The sweetest kangaroo who bounced up to you wanting attention. And loads of the colourful parrots everywhere. Good value for one night.
Great park friendly staff with a great atmosphere. Close to the beach, with good size sites.

We loved our short stay at Myola, highly recommend, booking the poolside cabin it was clean has air-conditioningfor the cooler months, tea,coffee & milk provided, it was very quiet & peaceful lots of wildlife around with a short walk to the beach or you can enjoy ferry ride to Huskisson for dinner or lunch.Definitely will be back.

Quiet caravan park. Mostly cabins with long termers
Nice to have a bathroom with no code too. Showers nice and hot and not timed Would be nice with just a little more power when turned on.
The people who look after the park are really great. Someone took our wood while we were away and they replaced for free, Overall. The place is great. Close to a beautiful beach that you can get to from the park. There is a spot undercover for barbecue and small area for kettles etc. A pool as well and play area for kids.

Everything seems good, service and the environment were good except there were thunderstorms at nights and heavy rain until the next day and our tent was flooded, we also had two ghost experiences. One was a woman in white at the empty cabin opposite to lot 30 unpowered, and the second one is the white figure walking past the firepit after we distinguished it. We packed up and left early because we had a little one with us. Overall, everything else was good.
Хорошее и тихое место, приходит семья кенгуру каждый вечер,но нет канализации для слива серой воды из каравана
We were placed nearby a permanent cabin that had people up drinking making noise all hours. Half of our site was taken up by a shrub so I wouldn't say it was well maintained. We were happy to leave.

Zuerst waren wir ein wenig skeptisch, da der Campingplatz schon ein wenig in dei Jahre gekommen ist. Allerdings hat er wirklich viel Charme. Überall sieht man Papageien uns Kängurus. Diese waren richtig zutraulich und haben Nachts vor unserem Zelt Grass gefressen. In der Abenddämmerung kann man auch auf einige Possums treffen.
Einen Stern Abzug, da es bei der Frauendusche abends immer kein warmes Wasser gab.
Le pire Holiday Park de mon voyage, très très sale, peu entretenu, pas d’espace cuisine…
Lovely beach. Friendly people. A bit run down but it fulfilled our needs

We booked a tent spot and were assigned an area that was not suitable for camping. The entire site was full of dust, with no grass or proper surface to set up a tent. We had no choice but to pitch our tent on this dusty ground, and we were lucky it didn’t rain, otherwise it would have been a disaster, likely turning mouldy and unbearable. This was very disappointing and far from what we expected.

On the positive side, the spot is very natural, and we were able to see kangaroos, which was a highlight. The closest beach, Callala, is a quiet and beautiful spot. This place might be better suited for campervans or booking a cabin, but it’s not ideal for tents. Also, note that it’s about 30 minutes away from Huskisson and other popular beaches, so be prepared for an hour of driving daily if you plan to explore those areas.
Stayed a night on an unpowered site, the staff were lovely.The bathrooms were very clean and sites were spacious.Walk to the beach was lovely and the birds around the park were beautiful.

Came here for a short stay with family. Cabin was spotlessly clean as was bedding and towels. Very comfortable and had everything we needed, even milk in the fridge which was great.  Short 5 minute walk to a lovely white sand beach and a little friendly Kangaroo family came to say hello by our cabin. Would recommend this site, as very quiet and friendly also. Would definitely stay again.

Save your pennies and stay at an M1 rest stop instead, you'll enjoy better views there. Full of permanent residents who are full of scowls and disapproving looks, don't expect to feel welcome. Negative 10 points to the guy that point blank walked up to a young woman and asked straight up if she was travelling alone and told her which caravan he lived in. Does not need to know. Negative 10 points to the shower that went absolutely freezing after 5 minutes despite no one else using it. Spent all my time walking back and forth because everything was so far away from my spot. $11 for laundry, which is extortionate. Super muddy and sandy and filthy, so look forward to cleaning your tent/van/room constantly. Kitchens probably need a dedicated fish cleaning station because all sinks covered in scales and guts. If you want to call them kitchens, pretty basic basic set of goods in an outdoor shelter. Would be miserable in winter. No sort of indoor shelter you can sit and charge a phone out of the weather unless you fancy hiding in the shower block and all sharing the one powerpoint available. Woken up by yapping dogs living in houses adjacent to the park which drove everyone nuts and horrible way to start the day. Watch out for the residents dog poo all over the place. Kangaroo poo they can't control, but dog poo they can disappointing. Can't wait to not come back.
Good park. The amenities were reasonably clean and we had a good time all round.
Prisvärt, bra mottagande och service. Lite äldre o slitet toalett mm Men ändå rent och väldigt bra känsla.

This was my first solo trip with my kids.
The place I booked was a lot smaller than I anticipated but to be honest I didn't put much effort into seeing the size when I booked it.
The area is great. Gave my kids so much confidence to walk around by themselves.
The staff were friendly and helpful as it was my first time in the area so I had no idea what was around.
The wifi was a little slow at times but it didn't affect us too much.
The BBQ area is great with a massive TV in there.
Only bummer was the pool not heated. I can only guess it was because we came in Autumn out of school holidays and running it would be a cost factor.
Will go back though. Definitely an adventure for us.
Sorry but more than one star isn't possible. It seems that everything there is more than 200 years old... It's possible that Captain Cook brought all this things from England...

Stayed in a camper. Beautiful quiet site in idyllic setting. Beaches are great at low tide. Staff and residents are really friendly. If you want to meet a family of kangaroos there's one here that are particularly friendly. Hot showers and amenities are great. Prices are incredibly affordable can't recommend it enough.

Wouldn't recommend camping by pitching tents here at all, may be getting a cabin is ok, if you pitch your tent on the tent sites on side it's just rows of cabins and on the other a huge a compound wall after which there are huge buildings again... definitely didn't feel like I went camping at all... unless you don't mind staying with 0 views or feels of camping.

Must say the toilets, baths were clean and the access to the beach is good. The receptionists were polite to answer and help.

Good campground. Nice facilities and very natural and quiet area. We saw a lot of animals and had a good time with all the kangaroos. We met some nice and kind persons here. The location is alright but it takes a long time to drive (by car) to huskisson and all the other places in the south

I am recommending this campground.

Cheers
Fantastic stay .
Lovely little caravan park with clean amenities and cheap! Wild kangaroos helping me cook was so funny 😂
Great place to own a small caravan annex  in this area just a short distance from everything you want to visit in the area.
Great place for access to a wonderful beach (not crowded when we went)

This is a beautiful holiday park. All facilities are well maintained. But the neighbours who smokes once every 5 minutes made our children and my family suffer. It is not suitable for non-smokers or children. To be honest, I want to come here on my next vacation, but unfortunately I don‘t think I can.

Callala & Myola locations are beautiful.. But the Tourist park has nothing to offer children other than a pool & a swing & location to the beach.
Pot holes through the park, the park looks dumpy & old.
With a big corporation owner, you  expect more.
It was our first camping holiday, so I have nothing to compare it to. Overall it was nice, the facilities were clean, and it has a decent pool and playground.

Not a bad place, didn’t get the cabin we ordered as it wasn’t cleaned so check in was 45 minutes later beach only five minutes walk quiet saltwater pool nice tennis courts just a shame not a shop close buy to get fresh bread or milk closes shop is 15 minutes drive witch is ok other than that will review and post after owe stay
Brilliant atmosphere

We rented a cabin, it was clean, the park was clean too. We enjoyed feeding the kangaroos the pellets we had left from the zoo (20mins away). The roads in the park are fine as long as you dont speed on them which you shouldn't anyway. The place was great for us and we will be back soon!

The walk to beach with the most white sand ive ever seen is 4 min walk away, the ferry is an experience with captain dave being very helpful and an awesome vibe in general. The ferry is very important when going to Huskisson as driving takes WAY longer, its worth the $15 return fare, trust me.

If you are staying in a cabin the wifi works okay, it gets you by but could be better. Possibly the cabins closer to the office have better wifi. The wifi didnt matter to me, we went to experience campfires, the wildlife and environmental features - and we did.

Had a sick time! Thanks heaps!

Great value place for the price. I booked a powered campervan place for the night. Toilets and showers are cleaned. Outside opening hours, you receive a code by sms to enter the site. Mine didn’t work but the owner is living nearby. Easy checkout. The beach is at 5min by work.
Beautiful location, lovely staff and good communication. Cabin was perfect - comfy beds, enough family space, all the facilities we needed. Quiet and peaceful
Gods Country 😍
Nos atendieron a pesar de que la recepción estaba cerrada. Muy buenas instalaciones, cerca de la playa. Hay que atravesar un pequeño bosque. El espacio para acampar es amplio.

Clean, Convenient, excellent service & value for money
Recommended affordable stay if your looking to come to Jervis Bay.
